title: The IP Context Object
description: The IP Context object summarizes all available information for an IP Address. It is provided as a single dictionary. All fields not explicitly marked as required may be omitted when the value is null.

ai.description: A top-level field describing AI activity that has been observed from this IP address.

operator.description: The name of the AI entity that is performing activity on this IP address.

types.description: The types of AI activity that have been observed from this IP address.

items.description: The different AI activity types observed from this IP address.

anyOf.description: AI activity that is performed by an AI agent.
AI activity that is performed by an AI crawler.

as.description: Spur IP GeoBGP autonomous system information.

organization.description: The organization that owns and operates the Autonomous System (AS) responsible for routing the IP address via BGP.
The organization currently assigned to use the specific IP address, such as a customer, hosting provider, or service operator.

client.description: Descriptive data about the connecting client.

behaviors.description: List of behaviors and usage detected for clients.

items.description: Describes client behaviors and usage activity.

anyOf.title: Service User
anyOf.description: A user of the provided service tag (e.g. 'TOR_PROXY_USER', 'EXAMPLE_USER').

concentration.description: Geographical and density information for clients on this IP Address.

density.description: A numerical measure of the client’s concentration (0-1).

skew.description: A measure representing point-to-point distance skew from the IP `location` field (KM).

state.description: Name or code of the state.

count.description: Number of instances or connections from this client.

countries.description: Number of unique countries the client connects from.

proxies.description: List of proxy services used by the client.

spread.description: An integer measuring the client spread (e.g., distance or distribution).

types.description: Types of devices or clients connecting (e.g., MOBILE, DESKTOP).

oneOf.description: Standard computers or laptops.
Devices/browsers without a standard display.
Connected devices such as game systems, smart TVs, streaming sticks, etc.
Mobile phones and tablets.
Generic datacenter or VPS environment.
Infrastructure for mobile networks (e.g., 4G/LTE).
Long range satellite-related infrastructure. IP Location is often distinct from physical location.
Infrastructure related to airline transit. IP Location is often distinct from physical location.
Infrastructure hosted by Google that pools and routes traffic from public wifi spaces and untrusted mobile networks.

ip.description: IPv4 address associated with the connection.

location.description: Spur IP Geo location information of the IP.

state.description: Name or code of the state.

risks.description: List of identified risk factors or behaviors.

oneOf.description: This IP has exhibited signs of committing ad fraud.
Network can route traffic for residential or malware proxies.
The datacenter or hosting location differs from the location of its users.
Persistent login attempts against web forms detected.
Exit point for an anonymizing VPN/proxy/tunnel. Traffic is likely associated with this service.
Automated or headless web scraping activity.

services.description: List of services or protocols in use (e.g., OPENVPN, IPSEC).

oneOf.description: Standard hypertext transfer protocol for web traffic.
Encrypted IP security protocol suite.
Layer 2 Tunneling Protocol for secure VPN connections.
Popular open-source VPN protocol.
Point-to-Point Tunneling Protocol for older VPN connections.
Secure proxy protocol built for censorship circumvention.
Proxy protocol that relays TCP/UDP traffic.
Encryption/transport protocol used by V2Ray.
Platform supporting encrypted traffic and multiple protocols.
Encrypted proxy protocol designed to imitate HTTPS.
Modern, high-performance VPN protocol.
Non-standard or private/closed-source protocol.

tunnels.description: Information about tunneling methods (VPN, TOR, etc.) used.

anonymous.description: Whether the tunnel operation is anonymized.

entries.description: Entry node IP addresses for the tunnel.

exits.description: Exit node IP addresses for the tunnel.

operator.description: Operator or vendor of the tunnel service.

type.description: Type of tunnel, e.g. VPN, TOR.
